metadata.prd_name: Ralph Meta-System: Self-Managing Task Queue & Ralph Decision Engine
metadata.objective: Create an intelligent system that decides when to use Ralph, manages incoming requests in a docket, and allows prioritization/sequencing of multi-request workflows

stories.title: Create Ralph Decision Engine (when to use Ralph)
stories.description: Build a decision tree/scoring system that analyzes user requests and determines: 1) Should this use Ralph?, 2) Which Ralph agent type?, 3) Autonomous or manual mode?, 4) Estimated story count. Output: ralph_decision.json with recommendation.
acceptance_criteria: Decision engine analyzes request complexity, scope, and type
Scores requests on: complexity (1-10), story count (1-20), parallelizability (yes/no)
Recommends: use_ralph (yes/no), agent_type (general/bash/etc), autonomous (yes/no)
Example: 'add business tracking' -> use_ralph=yes, stories=5, autonomous=yes
Example: 'fix typo' -> use_ralph=no (too simple)
Example: 'build entire system' -> use_ralph=yes, stories=15+, autonomous=yes with checkpoints
Outputs structured JSON decision for Claude to execute

stories.title: Build intelligent docket system (request queue)
stories.description: Create DOCKET.json system that captures new requests mid-workflow, prioritizes them, and allows sequential/parallel execution. User can interrupt current work with new request -> gets added to docket -> user chooses: tackle now or defer.
acceptance_criteria: DOCKET.json tracks: request_id, description, priority (urgent/high/normal/low), status (pending/in_progress/blocked/complete)
New request during work -> auto-added to docket with 'pending' status
User can say: 'add to docket for later' or 'tackle this now (pause current)'
python -m src.docket status -> shows all pending requests
python -m src.docket add 'description' --priority high
python -m src.docket next -> shows next recommended task
python -m src.docket complete [id] -> marks done, moves to next

stories.title: Create docket CLI and workflow integration
stories.description: Build CLI commands for docket management and integrate with Ralph. When user makes new request, Claude automatically: 1) Adds to docket, 2) Asks: 'Pause current work or add to queue?', 3) Executes based on choice.
acceptance_criteria: python -m src.docket status -> pretty-printed pending requests
python -m src.docket add 'request' --priority high -> adds to queue
python -m src.docket next -> recommends next task (by priority + dependencies)
python -m src.docket complete req_001 -> marks done
python -m src.docket block req_002 --reason 'waiting for X' -> sets blocked status
Integration: Claude checks docket at end of each task completion
Integration: New user request -> Claude asks: pause current or queue?
stories.notes: CHECKPOINT: Review docket system and Ralph decision engine before proceeding to meta-workflows

stories.title: Build Ralph auto-invocation system
stories.description: When user makes request, Claude automatically: 1) Runs ralph_decision_engine, 2) If use_ralph=yes, shows PRD preview, 3) Asks: 'Use Ralph for this? (recommended)', 4) Launches Ralph with generated PRD if yes.
acceptance_criteria: On user request, run: python -m src.ralph_decision_engine analyze 'request text'
If use_ralph=yes, generate PRD structure automatically
Show user: 'This looks like X stories. Use Ralph? (recommended)'
User says yes -> launch Ralph with auto-generated PRD
User says no -> Claude handles manually
Edge case: If already in Ralph context, add to current PRD as new story

auto_prd_generation.analyze_request: Use Claude to break down request into stories

stories.title: Create docket prioritization & dependency resolution
stories.description: Smart task ordering: dependency graph (req_002 depends on req_001), priority weighting, parallel execution detection (can req_003 and req_004 run simultaneously?). Output: optimized task sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Build dependency graph from docket entries
Detect circular dependencies (warn user)
python -m src.docket sequence -> shows optimized order
Identifies parallelizable tasks: 'req_003 and req_004 can run in parallel'
Respects priorities: urgent > high > normal > low
Blocked tasks excluded from sequence until unblocked
Example output: 'Next: req_001 (blocks 2 others), Then parallel: req_003 + req_004'

stories.title: Build meta-workflow: Ralph manages Ralph usage
stories.description: The ultimate meta: Ralph can CREATE PRDs for itself. User says 'optimize the docket system' -> Ralph analyzes docket system, generates PRD, asks: 'I created a 4-story plan to improve this. Run it?'
acceptance_criteria: Ralph can analyze existing code/systems
Ralph generates PRD for improvements
User approves PRD -> Ralph executes against itself
Example: 'Ralph, improve your decision engine' -> Ralph creates PRD to refactor ralph_decision_engine.py
Meta-circular: Ralph autonomously maintains its own systems
Safety: Always requires user approval before self-modification

self_improvement_flow.step_3: Ralph generates PRD with stories for improvements
self_improvement_flow.step_4: Ralph asks: 'I have a 5-story plan. Approve?'
self_improvement_flow.step_5: User approves -> Ralph executes PRD autonomously
self_improvement_flow.step_6: Ralph reports completion with before/after metrics

docket_integration_points.on_user_message: Check if new request -> add to docket
docket_integration_points.on_ralph_finish: Mark docket entry complete, show next
docket_integration_points.on_interrupt: Save current state, add to docket, handle new request

meta_circular_design.ralph_uses_ralph: Ralph decision engine uses Ralph for complex analysis
meta_circular_design.self_modifying: Ralph can improve its own code with user approval
meta_circular_design.recursive_improvement: Each Ralph execution can generate PRDs for next improvements

success_metrics.time_to_decision: <5 seconds to analyze request and recommend
success_metrics.user_satisfaction: User says 'yes' to Ralph recommendation 90%+ of time
